pull_request.title: Add server-side orchestration engine with event sourcing

canonical_items.title:
Non-atomic event appending can corrupt state on retry. If an error occurs mid-loop (lines 96-102) after some events are persisted but before the receipt is written, the command appears to fail. A retry generates new UUIDs via `crypto.randomUUID()` in the decider, appending duplicate events. Consider wrapping the loop in a transaction or using deterministic event IDs derived from `commandId`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

A dispatch error in `processEvent` will terminate the `Effect.forever` loop, permanently halting event ingestion. Consider adding error recovery (e.g., `Effect.catchAll` with logging) around `processEvent` so failures don't kill the fiber.

Consider attaching a no-op error listener before `socket.write` (e.g., `socket.on('error', () => {})`) to prevent an unhandled `EPIPE`/`ECONNRESET` from crashing the process if the client disconnects mid-handshake.
Build script runs TypeScript file with bare node
Build script uses `node` to run `.ts` file directly
Inconsistent input normalization across CheckpointStore methods

Git's braced rename syntax (e.g., `src/{old => new}/file.ts`) isn't handled correctly. The current slice after ` => ` produces invalid paths like `new}/file.ts`. Consider expanding the braces to construct the full destination path.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

`loadCustomKeybindingsConfig` fails when the config file doesn't exist, which is expected for new users. Consider catching `ENOENT` and returning an empty array instead.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
Template literal `${cause}` throws if `cause` is a `Symbol`. Consider using `String(cause)` instead.
Forked revert dispatch risks read model inconsistency

If `projectionPipeline.projectEvent` fails after `eventStore.append` succeeds, the event is persisted but `readModel` isn't updated, causing desync. Consider updating the in-memory `readModel` immediately after append (before the external projection), so local state stays consistent regardless of downstream failures.
Engine error handler catches all errors including non-invariant ones

The gap-filling fallback logic can retain messages from turns that are about to be deleted, causing foreign key violations. Consider removing the fallback logic entirely, or filtering `fallbackUserMessages` and `fallbackAssistantMessages` to only include messages whose `turnId` is in `retainedTurnIds`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

The in-memory `pendingTurnStartByThreadId` map isn't restored during bootstrap. If the service restarts after processing `thread.turn-start-requested` but before `thread.session-set`, the `userMessageId` and `startedAt` will be lost since bootstrap resumes *after* the committed sequence. Consider persisting this pending state or processing these two events atomically.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

`REQUIRED_SNAPSHOT_PROJECTORS` includes `pending-approvals` and `thread-turns`, but `getSnapshot` doesn't query their data. If these projectors lag behind, the returned `snapshotSequence` will be lower than what the included data actually reflects, causing clients to replay already-applied events. Consider filtering `REQUIRED_SNAPSHOT_PROJECTORS` to only include projectors whose data is actually fetched in the snapshot.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
Unbounded memory growth in turn start deduplication set
ProviderRuntimeIngestion processes events for wrong thread on race

On `message.completed`, the message ID is added to the set and `thread.message.assistant.complete` is dispatched. On `turn.completed`, the same set is iterated and `thread.message.assistant.complete` is dispatched again for each ID—including already-completed ones. Consider removing message IDs from the set after dispatching on `message.completed`, or filtering out already-completed IDs before the `turn.completed` loop.

Fish shell outputs `$PATH` as space-separated, not colon-separated. Consider checking if the shell is fish and using `string join : $PATH` instead, or validating the result contains colons before assigning.

Using `-il` flags causes the shell to source profile scripts that may print banners or other text, polluting the captured `PATH`. Consider using `-lc` (login only, non-interactive) to reduce unwanted output.

In `executeUnprepared`, preparing outside an effect and not closing the statement can cause uncaught errors and leaks. Suggest wrapping with `Effect.acquireUseRelease`: prepare in acquire (error-safe) and `finalize` in release.
Consider adding `.catch(() => {})` after `Effect.runPromise(handleMessage(ws, raw))` to prevent unhandled rejections from crashing the server if `encodeResponse` or setup logic fails.
WS message handler can cause unhandled promise rejection
Branch sync dispatches both server and stale local update

`parseFileUrlHref` already decodes the path (line 46), but `safeDecode` is called again here, corrupting filenames containing `%` sequences. Consider skipping the decode when `fileUrlTarget` is non-null.

`EXTERNAL_SCHEME_PATTERN` matches `script.ts:10` as a scheme because `.ts:` looks like `scheme:`. Consider requiring `://` after the colon, or checking that what follows the colon is not just digits.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
Dummy workflow marker accidentally committed to docs
Three error classes defined but never instantiated anywhere
Redundant `CheckpointInvariantError` in `CheckpointServiceError` union type
Redundant error type in CheckpointServiceError union definition

`Effect.callback` should return a cleanup function to close the server(s) on fiber interruption. Without it, the `Net.Server` handles keep the process alive and leak the port if the effect is cancelled.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

Multi-byte UTF-8 characters split across chunks will be corrupted when decoding each chunk separately. Consider accumulating all chunks first, then decoding once, or use `TextDecoder` with `stream: true`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

The `+` key can be parsed (via trailing `+` handling) but cannot be encoded because `shortcut.key.includes("+")` returns true for the literal `+` key. Consider checking `shortcut.key === "+"` separately and encoding it as `"space"` style (e.g., a special token), or adjusting the condition to allow the single `+` character.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

`upsertKeybindingRule` has a race condition: concurrent calls read the same file state, then the last write overwrites earlier changes. Consider wrapping the read-modify-write sequence with `Effect.Semaphore` to serialize access.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
Template literal interpolation throws `TypeError` if `cause` is a `Symbol`. Consider using `String(cause)` for safe coercion.
Duplicated `resolveThreadWorkspaceCwd` across three files
Duplicated workspace CWD resolution logic across reactor modules
Checkpoint reactor swallows diff errors silently for `turn.completed`

`truncateDetail` slices to `limit - 1` then appends `"..."` (3 chars), producing strings of length `limit + 2`. Consider slicing to `limit - 3` instead.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

`latestMessageIdByTurnKey` is written to but never read, and `clearAssistantMessageIdsForTurn` doesn't clear its entries—only `clearTurnStateForSession` does. Consider removing this map entirely if unused, or clearing it alongside `turnMessageIdsByTurnKey` in `clearAssistantMessageIdsForTurn`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

`SqlSchema.findOneOption` can produce both SQL errors and decode errors, but `mapError` wraps all as `PersistenceSqlError`. Consider distinguishing `ParseError` from SQL errors and mapping decode failures to `PersistenceDecodeError` instead.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

`statement.run()` returns a `RunResult` object, not an array. Casting it to `ReadonlyArray<any>` will cause runtime errors when consumers call array methods. Consider wrapping the result (e.g., `[result]`) or returning a properly typed object.

`JSON.stringify(cause)` returns `undefined` for `undefined`, functions, or symbols, violating the `string` return type. Consider coercing the result to a string (e.g., `String(JSON.stringify(cause))`) or adding a fallback.

The read-modify-write pattern (`getBySessionId` → merge → `upsert`) is susceptible to lost updates under concurrent writes. Consider wrapping in a transaction or adding optimistic concurrency control (e.g., version field) if concurrent session updates are expected.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

Using `??` for `providerThreadId` and `adapterKey` makes it impossible to clear these fields by passing `null`, since `null ?? existing` evaluates to `existing`. Consider using explicit `undefined` checks (like `resumeCursor` does) if clearing should be supported.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

Race condition: `processHandle` may be `null` when `data` callback fires, since it's assigned after `Bun.spawn` returns. Consider initializing `BunPtyProcess` first, then passing it to the callback to avoid losing initial output.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
Consider using `socket.end(response)` instead of `socket.write(response)` + `socket.destroy()` to ensure the HTTP error response is fully flushed before closing the connection.

When array chunks contain a multi-byte UTF-8 character split across boundaries, decoding each chunk separately produces replacement characters. Consider using `Buffer.concat()` on all chunks before calling `.toString("utf8")`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

When `onOpenChange` is provided without `open`, the internal `_open` state never updates because `setOpenProp` takes precedence. Consider calling `_setOpen` when `openProp === undefined`, regardless of whether `setOpenProp` exists.

The `resizable` object is recreated on every render, causing `SidebarRail`'s `useEffect` to repeatedly read localStorage and update the DOM. Consider memoizing the object with `useMemo`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

When `localStorage.getItem()` returns `null`, `Number(null)` evaluates to `0`, which passes `Number.isFinite(0)`. This causes the sidebar to clamp to `minWidth` on first load, overriding the `DIFF_INLINE_DEFAULT_WIDTH` CSS clamp. Consider checking for `null` or empty string before parsing, e.g. guard with `storedWidth === null || storedWidth === ''`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

Suggestion: don’t spread `params` into `body`; it can override `_tag` and mishandle non-object values. Keep `_tag` separate and nest `params` under a single key (e.g., `data`), or validate `params` is a plain object.

`defaultModel` should be `Schema.optional(Schema.NullOr(Schema.String))` to allow clearing the value. Currently there's no way to reset it to `null` since omitting means "no change" in patch semantics.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

threads.author: greptile-apps
threads.title: calls can execute concurrently for the same thread, causing out-of-order event processing. If two provider events arrive rapidly (e.g., textDelta then turn/completed), the commands may reach the queue in unpredictable order. While the Effect queue serializes command processing, the async IIFE allows the event handler to return immediately and process the next provider event before previous dispatches complete. This can cause logical inconsistencies in message streaming or session state updates. Consider wrapping the entire event handler in a per-thread mutex or processing provider events sequentially.
threads.phase0_reason: Thread is flagged outdated by GitHub diff context.

threads.author: greptile-apps
threads.title: returns the current in-memory read model, but it may not reflect recently dispatched commands that are still queued. If a provider event arrives before the previous command's read model update propagates, `thread` lookup may fail or use stale data. This is especially problematic during rapid event streams where session creation and message events arrive in quick succession.
threads.phase0_reason: Thread is flagged outdated by GitHub diff context.

threads.author: greptile-apps
threads.title: catches an error during event processing (line 201-205), it creates a new Error and calls `envelope.reject()`, but the original error is not logged. This makes debugging event processing failures very difficult. Since the `catch` callback returns `undefined`, the stream continues processing subsequent commands even after failures, which may lead to inconsistent state if error recovery is needed.
threads.phase0_reason: Thread is flagged outdated by GitHub diff context.

threads.author: greptile-apps
threads.title: runs PubSub publishes in parallel with synchronous listener callbacks (lines 193-198), but the listeners fire before waiting for PubSub to complete. If PubSub publish fails, listeners already received the update, creating inconsistency between Effect subscribers and direct listeners. The envelope is also resolved (line 199) before verifying both PubSub publishes succeeded.
threads.phase0_reason: Thread is flagged outdated by GitHub diff context.

threads.author: greptile-apps
threads.title: is false and `message.text.length > 0`, the entire text is replaced (line 150). However, if `message.text` is empty string and `streaming` is false (like the turn/completed event at wsServer.ts:198), it preserves `entry.text` (line 152). This creates an edge case: if a streaming message never sent any text deltas, then receives a finalizing empty-text event, the message keeps empty text from initialization instead of the intended final state.
threads.phase0_reason: Thread is flagged outdated by GitHub diff context.

threads.author: coderabbitai
threads.title: command (SendMessageCommandSchema) omits attachments—only text is sent to orchestration. While attachments are correctly forwarded to the provider API separately (line 1752), the composer images are cleared immediately (line 1723) before attachment metadata can be persisted to the message itself. Since OrchestrationMessageSchema has no attachments field and the read-model's messages derive from orchestration events, user images will vanish on page refresh or if the read-model becomes the authoritative source. To preserve attachments: either add attachment metadata to SendMessageCommandSchema and the message reducer, or maintain a local optimistic state mapping userMessageId → attachments until the read-model is updated to include attachment data. <details> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

threads.author: coderabbitai
threads.title: with a Bun-compatible SQLite driver.** The `node:sqlite` module is a Node.js 22.6+ built-in that Bun does not support. CI runs exclusively on Bun 1.3.9, and `OrchestrationEngine` (which uses `SqliteEventStore`) is imported in `wsServer.ts` and tested in `engine.test.ts`. When `bun run test` executes in CI, it will fail to load this code with "No such built-in module: node:sqlite". Switch to `bun:sqlite` (which is built-in to Bun) or add a packaged SQLite driver (e.g., `better-sqlite3`, `sql.js`) to apps/server dependencies and abstract the driver behind an interface for portability. <details> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

threads.author: coderabbitai
threads.title: with `catchAll` to avoid catching defects.** Using `catchAllCause` will catch both expected errors and defects (bugs). Per project conventions, use `catchAll` or `mapError` instead so that genuine bugs propagate as failures rather than being silently caught and turned into user-facing errors. <details> <summary>🛡️ Proposed fix</summary>
threads.phase0_reason: Thread is flagged outdated by GitHub diff context.

threads.author: coderabbitai
threads.title: from DB operations—it masks expected failures as defects.** `JSON.parse` and `Schema.decodeUnknownSync` can throw expected errors (invalid payloads, malformed JSON). Converting these to defects via `orDie` crashes orchestration flows instead of handling them predictably. Update `OrchestrationEventRepositoryShape` to return typed errors (e.g., `Effect.Effect<OrchestrationEvent, ParseError | ValidationError>`) and remove `orDie` from both `append` and `readFromSequence`. <details> <summary>Suggested starting point</summary>

threads.author: coderabbitai
threads.title: 💡 Result: In **Effect (TypeScript)**, `Effect.try` has two overloads—one that takes just a thunk, and one that takes an object with `try` and a `catch` handler. The **`catch` handler signature** is: - `catch: (error: unknown) => E` (i.e., it receives the thrown value as `unknown` and must map it to your typed error `E`). [1] Type definition (from the docs):

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title: After `providerManager.startSession` returns, the `sessionId` is never dispatched to `OrchestrationEngine`, so `thread.session?.sessionId` remains unset. The `providerManager.on("event")` listener then fails to find the thread and silently drops all streaming events. Consider dispatching a `thread.session` command after `startSession` to link the session with the thread. ✅ Resolved in af53ae8b4845852a320f108dda30b32afc3c4029

threads.author: coderabbitai
threads.title: exists, but the race condition persists: between the `findProjectByCwd` check and `insertProject` call, a concurrent request can insert the same `cwd`, causing the insert to fail. The `.pipe(Effect.orDie)` converts this constraint violation into an unhandled error instead of gracefully returning the existing project. Under concurrent load, this violates the coding guideline to "maintain predictable behavior under load and during failures." Wrap the insert in error handling to catch the constraint violation and re-fetch the existing row, or use a database upsert/on-conflict pattern to ensure atomic, race-free behavior. <details> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
The in-memory `pendingTurnStartByThreadId` map isn't restored during bootstrap. If the service restarts after processing `thread.turn-start-requested` but before `thread.session-set`, the `userMessageId` and `startedAt` will be lost since bootstrap resumes *after* the committed sequence. Consider persisting this pending state or processing these two events atomically.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
Using `??` for `providerThreadId` and `adapterKey` makes it impossible to clear these fields by passing `null`, since `null ?? existing` evaluates to `existing`. Consider using explicit `undefined` checks (like `resumeCursor` does) if clearing should be supported.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
`upsertKeybindingRule` has a race condition: concurrent calls read the same file state, then the last write overwrites earlier changes. Consider wrapping the read-modify-write sequence with `Effect.Semaphore` to serialize access.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
The `+` key can be parsed (via trailing `+` handling) but cannot be encoded because `shortcut.key.includes("+")` returns true for the literal `+` key. Consider checking `shortcut.key === "+"` separately and encoding it as `"space"` style (e.g., a special token), or adjusting the condition to allow the single `+` character.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
`loadCustomKeybindingsConfig` fails when the config file doesn't exist, which is expected for new users. Consider catching `ENOENT` and returning an empty array instead.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
`latestMessageIdByTurnKey` is written to but never read, and `clearAssistantMessageIdsForTurn` doesn't clear its entries—only `clearTurnStateForSession` does. Consider removing this map entirely if unused, or clearing it alongside `turnMessageIdsByTurnKey` in `clearAssistantMessageIdsForTurn`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title: When `onOpenChange` is provided without `open`, the internal `_open` state never updates because `setOpenProp` takes precedence. Consider calling `_setOpen` when `openProp === undefined`, regardless of whether `setOpenProp` exists.
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
`truncateDetail` slices to `limit - 1` then appends `"..."` (3 chars), producing strings of length `limit + 2`. Consider slicing to `limit - 3` instead.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
Non-atomic event appending can corrupt state on retry. If an error occurs mid-loop (lines 96-102) after some events are persisted but before the receipt is written, the command appears to fail. A retry generates new UUIDs via `crypto.randomUUID()` in the decider, appending duplicate events. Consider wrapping the loop in a transaction or using deterministic event IDs derived from `commandId`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
Race condition: `processHandle` may be `null` when `data` callback fires, since it's assigned after `Bun.spawn` returns. Consider initializing `BunPtyProcess` first, then passing it to the callback to avoid losing initial output.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
Git's braced rename syntax (e.g., `src/{old => new}/file.ts`) isn't handled correctly. The current slice after ` => ` produces invalid paths like `new}/file.ts`. Consider expanding the braces to construct the full destination path.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title:
Multi-byte UTF-8 characters split across chunks will be corrupted when decoding each chunk separately. Consider accumulating all chunks first, then decoding once, or use `TextDecoder` with `stream: true`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.

threads.author: macroscopeapp
threads.title: On `message.completed`, the message ID is added to the set and `thread.message.assistant.complete` is dispatched. On `turn.completed`, the same set is iterated and `thread.message.assistant.complete` is dispatched again for each ID—including already-completed ones. Consider removing message IDs from the set after dispatching on `message.completed`, or filtering out already-completed IDs before the `turn.completed` loop.
threads.phase0_reason: Canonical actionable item.
